chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD 1st Appeal No.D-18 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 22.04.2026 Mr. Muzamil Khan, advocate for appellant *** Zubair Ameer S/o Ameer-ud-Din, the appellant-customer is aggrieved by the IInd Banking Court Hyderabad’s judgment and final decree dated 03.02.2026 passed in banking Suit No.153 of 2024. The principle challenge articulated before us today by the appellant’s counsel is that the appellant – customer is/was suffering from blood cancer, fell ill and suffered business losses. Hence, he could not meet the payment obligations and consequently was unable to fulfil his financial obligations. Counsel contends that today the appellant is poorer. He submits that appellant is ready and willing to pay the entire decreetal amount but seeks repayment by way of either instalments or by way of deferment and/or seeks mercy of this bench on account of the circumstances mentioned above. Heard counsel. With great respect, the submissions pleaded cannot be considered as valid grounds for admission of this first appeal or for that matter to even issue notice to respondent bank – Bank Al-Falah Limited (“BAFL”). The appellant’s plea may be agitated before the Executing Court where the matter may be considered on its own merit, subject to all legal consequences. In view of the above, this first appeal is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali
